task: DP1-REOPEN Part 2 -- recompute f (the China-NATIONALITY fraction) against the N-PORT panel. RESULT: f remains UNDETERMINED because nationality is the commercially-gated HOLE. Compute ONLY what the RESIDENCE panel supports, labelled explicitly as NOT f.
scope_limit: Wrote EXACTLY this file plus build/audit/sep_retest_nationality.json (Part 3) and the shared recompute script build/audit/sep_retest_nationality_recompute.py. Did NOT modify the operator, contracts, dp3/dp4/dp5/sep_* prior artifacts, china_fraction_bound.json, f_pin_test.json, or the panel. DP6 NOT started. No hazard, no date, no bare probability.
read_only_inputs: build/data/nport/us_china_panel_provenance.json (provenance: subset disclosure, 99.33% identifier coverage, the nationality HOLE)
build/model/china_fraction_bound.json + build/audit/f_pin_test.json (the pinned f in [0.49,0.71], issuance/holdings-side, frozen-2020 GCAP vintage)

HEADLINE_DETERMINATION.claim: The China-NATIONALITY fraction f -- the share of US-held offshore/haven (CYM/HKG/VGB) exposure that reattributes to China by ULTIMATE PARENT -- CANNOT be measured from the N-PORT panel. f remains UNDETERMINED.
HEADLINE_DETERMINATION.SUBJECT_DRIVER: f is a NATIONALITY (ultimate-parent) quantity; the panel's only geographic field, investment_country (N-PORT item C.5.a), is RESIDENCE (issuer country of organization). The driver of the non-measurability is that the haven->ultimate-parent reattribution is not in N-PORT and the issuer_nationality column is uniformly 'UNDETERMINED-NO-PUBLIC-CROSSWALK' on all 1,141,109 rows (verified, see verification.issuer_nationality_distinct_values).
HEADLINE_DETERMINATION.BOUNDARIES: Holds for the N-PORT public data (US-registered-fund holders only, RESIDENCE basis, 8-fiscal-quarter subset 2019q3..2024q4). It is NOT a claim that f is unmeasurable in principle -- it is measurable once a licensed CMNS ultimate-parent crosswalk is applied to this exact panel (see crosswalk_that_would_close_it).
HEADLINE_DETERMINATION.FALSIFIER: A reproducible PUBLIC issuer->ultimate-parent-nationality crosswalk applied to the panel that fills issuer_nationality with non-UNDETERMINED China/non-China assignments at >=95% row coverage, yielding a measured f. None exists at this granularity: the GCAP/CMNS crosswalk requires 7 licensed, non-redistributed commercial ownership databases (CGS, Dealogic, SDC, S&P Capital IQ, Bureau van Dijk ORBIS, FactSet, Morningstar) plus an OpenFIGI key.
HEADLINE_DETERMINATION.SOURCE: build/data/nport/us_china_nationality_panel.parquet (issuer_nationality column, recomputed value_counts on disk: single value 'UNDETERMINED-NO-PUBLIC-CROSSWALK'); build/contracts/nport_gcap_contract.json structure.crosswalk_determination and known_frictions; build/data/nport/us_china_panel_provenance.json NATIONALITY_IS_UNDETERMINED_HOLE.

f_is_not_fabricated: No quarterly f is produced. The panel cannot distinguish a China-parent haven-issued security (e.g. Alibaba, residence KY) from a genuinely-haven security, so any 'f' derived here would be a relabelled residence statistic -- a substitution this build explicitly guards against. f stays UNDETERMINED.

RESIDENCE_STATISTICS_EXPLICITLY_NOT_F.what_this_is: USD value (N-PORT item C.2.c) of US-registered-fund holdings of CN-RESIDENT issuers vs CYM/HKG/VGB-RESIDENT issuers, per fiscal quarter, and the RESIDENCE ratio CN-resident / (CN-resident + haven-resident). This is a RESIDENCE statistic. It is NOT the nationality fraction f.
RESIDENCE_STATISTICS_EXPLICITLY_NOT_F.why_it_is_NOT_f_SUBJECT_DRIVER: The residence ratio measures CN-resident mass relative to haven-resident mass. f measures the China-by-ULTIMATE-PARENT share OF the haven-resident mass. The residence ratio OMITS exactly the China-parent haven-issued securities (Alibaba-type, residence KY) that f is built to capture -- those sit entirely in the haven-resident denominator term, attributed away from China. So the residence ratio is a LOWER-bound-flavoured residence statistic, NOT the reattribution fraction f; the two answer different questions and are not comparable as levels.
RESIDENCE_STATISTICS_EXPLICITLY_NOT_F.value_basis_caveat_SOURCE: currency_value is N-PORT item C.2.c summed in its reported units. Per build/contracts/nport_gcap_contract.json (value_basis: PARTIALLY VERIFIED) and the provenance value_basis_caveat, USD denomination of currency_value is the N-PORT standard but was NOT confirmed from the primary form-instruction text (SEC instruction PDF returned 403 in Part 0); currency_code is mixed across rows (USD 36.1%, HKD, CNY, ...), retained per row so denomination is auditable. The residence statistics below are sums of C.2.c as reported; they are labelled USD on the N-PORT standard but carry this UNVERIFIED-denomination caveat. ~0.94% of rows carry a negative currency_value (short/derivative legs), included as-filed.

residence_trend_2019q3_to_2024q4.SUBJECT_DRIVER: The OBSERVABLE residence ratio CN-resident/(CN-resident+haven-resident) declines mildly across the 8-quarter subset, driven by haven-resident mass (CYM+HKG+VGB) growing faster than CN-resident mass over 2019->2024.
residence_trend_2019q3_to_2024q4.shape: Roughly flat ~0.30 through 2020q3 (peak 0.314 at 2020q3), then steps down to ~0.27-0.28 in 2022 and ~0.25 by 2024q4. Monotone-ish DOWN after 2020q3 on this subset.
residence_trend_2019q3_to_2024q4.BOUNDARIES: 8 documented fiscal quarters (subset, NOT the continuous 21-quarter range); US-registered-fund holders only; RESIDENCE basis; mixed-currency C.2.c sums with the UNVERIFIED-USD-denomination caveat.
residence_trend_2019q3_to_2024q4.FALSIFIER: Recompute group-sums of currency_value by fiscal_quarter x investment_country_iso3 from the panel and obtain a first/last residence ratio differing from 0.2999 / 0.2505 (delta -0.049). Reproduced by build/audit/sep_retest_nationality_recompute.py from disk.
residence_trend_2019q3_to_2024q4.explicitly_not_nationality_f: This DOWN drift is a RESIDENCE trend. It says nothing directly about whether the NATIONALITY f rose or fell: a falling residence-CN share is consistent with MORE China-parent issuance migrating INTO the havens (which would RAISE nationality f while LOWERING the residence-CN ratio) just as easily as with less China exposure. The sign of the nationality f trend is UNOBSERVABLE here. Do not read the residence decline as a decline in f.

COMPARISON_TO_PINNED_INTERVAL.claim: The pinned f in [0.49,0.71] (from f_pin_test.json / china_fraction_bound.json) can be NEITHER CONFIRMED NOR REFUTED from public N-PORT data.
COMPARISON_TO_PINNED_INTERVAL.SUBJECT_DRIVER: The pin is a NATIONALITY fraction (GCAP US-holdings-side restatement, haven->CHN ultimate parent, vintage 2007-2020). The N-PORT panel is RESIDENCE-basis with nationality UNDETERMINED, so it cannot produce a nationality f to test the pin against. The residence ratio (~0.25-0.31) is NOT comparable to the pinned nationality interval -- different denominators, different question (see RESIDENCE_STATISTICS_EXPLICITLY_NOT_F.why_it_is_NOT_f).
COMPARISON_TO_PINNED_INTERVAL.do_not_relabel: The residence ratio ~0.25-0.31 must NOT be read as a low-side estimate of f that 'refutes' [0.49,0.71]. It is a residence statistic that omits the haven-issued China-parent securities; the nationality f restates those very securities to China and is expected to be HIGHER than any residence-CN ratio. Treating the residence ratio as f would be the exact substitution this build guards against.
COMPARISON_TO_PINNED_INTERVAL.BOUNDARIES: Applies to the public N-PORT panel only. The pin's own vintage caveat (2020-vintage GCAP, DP2 cell is 2025-S1) is untouched here.
COMPARISON_TO_PINNED_INTERVAL.FALSIFIER: Produce a nationality-resolved f from the panel (requires the licensed crosswalk) and compare to [0.49,0.71]. Not possible from public data in this pass.
COMPARISON_TO_PINNED_INTERVAL.SOURCE: build/audit/f_pin_test.json (f_interval_pinned [0.49,0.71], US-holdings-side nationality restatement); build/data/nport/us_china_nationality_panel.parquet (residence basis, nationality UNDETERMINED).

REPLACES_VINTAGE_LEAP.claim: This Part REPLACES any 'vintage-leap' assumption (extrapolating the 2020-vintage nationality f forward to 2024/2025) with the honest statement: f is STILL UNMEASURABLE AT NATIONALITY GRANULARITY from public data.
REPLACES_VINTAGE_LEAP.SUBJECT_DRIVER: The reopening hoped N-PORT would supply a post-2020 nationality-basis f. It does not: N-PORT is residence-basis and the nationality column is the HOLE. So the post-2020 nationality f is not measured here; it remains pinned at its 2020 vintage with the unverified-2025 caveat carried in f_pin_test.json -- not refreshed by N-PORT.
REPLACES_VINTAGE_LEAP.not_dressed_as_a_measurement: This is reported as 'still unmeasurable at nationality granularity', NOT as a fresh measurement of f. The only NEW measured quantities are the RESIDENCE statistics above, which are labelled NOT f.
REPLACES_VINTAGE_LEAP.BOUNDARIES: Public N-PORT, 8-quarter subset, US-fund holders, residence basis.
REPLACES_VINTAGE_LEAP.FALSIFIER: A public nationality-resolved f at any post-2020 vintage. None obtained.
REPLACES_VINTAGE_LEAP.SOURCE: build/audit/f_pin_test.json residual_gaps_carried_as_unverified (VINTAGE); build/contracts/nport_gcap_contract.json crosswalk_determination; this panel.

currency_code_mix_pct.note: remainder HKD/CNY/TWD/EUR/... ; C.2.c value-denomination caveat applies

verification.reproducibility: Every residence number above is regenerated from build/data/nport/us_china_nationality_panel.parquet alone by the recompute script (part2_residence_per_quarter / part2_residence_trend). The HOLE assertion is a value_counts on issuer_nationality returning the single UNDETERMINED value.

verdict_part2: f UNDETERMINED. The nationality fraction f is NOT measured and NOT fabricated -- nationality is the commercially-gated HOLE (issuer_nationality uniformly UNDETERMINED). The only computed quantities are RESIDENCE statistics, labelled NOT f: per-quarter CN-resident vs haven-resident USD holdings and the RESIDENCE ratio CN/(CN+haven), which drifts DOWN from 0.300 (2019q3) to 0.250 (2024q4), delta -0.049. That residence DOWN trend is explicitly distinguished from the unobservable nationality-f trend. The pinned nationality f in [0.49,0.71] can be neither confirmed nor refuted from public N-PORT data.
